content.theme: Everyone else avoids pain. You collect it. Your body is a filter — suffering flows in from your crew and out onto the enemy. The cost is always yours.
content.design: Sin Eater is the "caster" support class. Every ability has a cost — Toxicity, HP, or carrying conditions. Two branches — Martyr (absorb/cleanse/heal) and Vessel (apply DoTs, redirect conditions, debuff enemies) — share the same tree and feed each other. Toxicity is your mana pool: it fuels your power but kills you if it overflows.

content.label: Critical Rule
content.text: Sin Eater abilities never deal direct damage. They move conditions, remove conditions, apply conditions (Bleed, Poison, Burn, Hex), and heal — but the Sin Eater never rolls a damage die through their tree abilities. All offensive output is DoT through condition application. If they want burst damage, they use a weapon like everyone else.

content.label: Key Differences
content.text: • vs Influence: Influence shapes the fight through words — buffs and debuffs that cost AP but no personal risk. Sin Eater shapes the fight through sacrifice — every ability costs you something physical. Influence is safe support. Sin Eater is dangerous support. • vs Leech: Leech mainlines Toxicity for personal offensive power — selfish, aggressive, burns hot. Sin Eater mainlines Toxicity for squad survival and DoT pressure — selfless, reactive. Same fuel, opposite purpose. They synergize: the Leech runs hot, the Sin Eater acts as their overflow valve.
Core Mechanics
Toxicity as Mana

Toxicity is the Sin Eater's central resource. Most abilities either generate Toxicity (Martyr branch — absorbing and healing costs you) or spend Toxicity (Vessel branch — applying DoTs and redirecting consumes your accumulated contamination). The Sin Eater wants to stay in a sweet spot: enough Toxicity to fuel Vessel abilities, not so much that penalties bury you.

rows: Danger zone. -2 to all checks. -1 HP per round.
Critical. -3 to all checks. -2 HP per round. Mutation risk on any Scar.
Collapse. Unconscious. Gain a Scar. Toxicity resets to 15.

content.text: The Sin Eater lives in the 5–10 range by design. Dip into the warning zone to fuel big plays, dump it back out through Vessel abilities before it climbs higher.
The Four Plates

Plate 1 — Your Toxicity: Goes up when you Absorb or heal. Goes down when you Redirect or spend it on Vessel abilities. Stay in the sweet spot. Plate 2 — Ally Conditions: Every round enemies apply conditions to your squad. You triage: what MUST you eat now, what can they survive for one more round? Plate 3 — Your Conditions: Everything you Absorbed is now on YOU. Bleed is ticking. Stagger is limiting your AP. You need to Redirect or Purge before they bury you. Plate 4 — Your HP: Some abilities cost HP directly. You're squishy (Interface/Edge). Every HP spent is a step closer to a Scar.
Martyr Trigger

Use an ability that removes a condition from an ally or heals an ally. Martyr augments activate when you sacrifice for your team. The more you absorb and heal, the more Toxicity you accumulate — and the more fuel you have for Vessel abilities.
Vessel Trigger

Use an ability that applies a condition to an enemy, spends Toxicity offensively, or redirects a condition onto an enemy. Vessel augments activate when you weaponize your accumulated suffering. All Vessel offensive output is DoT — Bleed, Poison, Burn, Hex. No burst. No direct damage dice.
Combined

Absorb a condition from an ally (Martyr — Toxicity goes up) → Redirect that condition onto an enemy (Vessel — condition leaves you, enemy starts ticking). Or: heal an ally (Martyr — Toxicity goes up) → spend Toxicity to apply Burn to an enemy (Vessel — Toxicity goes down). You're a pump. Suffering flows through you. The faster you cycle, the more effective you are.

content.label: Natural Counters
content.text: • AoE/persistent damage — if the whole squad takes conditions every round, you can't Absorb fast enough • Anti-healing effects — anything that prevents condition removal shuts down Martyr • Toxicity-boosting enemies — rift creatures that apply Toxicity directly push your mana bar toward the ceiling • Condition-immune enemies — constructs, certain bosses immune to Bleed/Poison negate Vessel's offensive output • Focus fire on the Sin Eater — you're squishy and actively spending your own HP • Isolation — anything that separates you from allies prevents Absorb

augments.name: Empathic Link
augments.effect: Unlocks Absorb mechanic. 1 AP, Close range: remove one condition from an ally and take it onto yourself at full strength. +1 Toxicity per Absorb. The foundation of everything.

augments.name: Rift Metabolizer
augments.effect: When you Absorb a condition from an ally, heal them for 1 HP. Pulling suffering out mends a little of what it caused.

augments.name: Afflict Touch
augments.effect: 1 AP, Close range, Interface check vs target's Grit: apply one minor DoT/debuff — Bleed 1 (1 damage/round for 2 rounds), Poison 1 (1 damage/round, stacks), or Dull (-1 Evasion for 1 round). +1 Toxicity.

augments.name: Toxicity Sense
augments.effect: You can see Toxicity levels, active conditions, and HP ranges on all creatures within Medium range. You always know who's hurting and how badly.

augments.name: Pain Threshold
augments.effect: You can carry up to 3 conditions before overload penalties (normally 2). Your body is built for this.

augments.name: Sympathetic Healing
augments.effect: When you Absorb a condition from an ally, heal them for 1d4 + Interface bonus HP. Replaces Rift Metabolizer's flat 1 HP (take the higher value).

augments.name: Transfer Efficiency
augments.effect: First Absorb each turn costs +0 Toxicity instead of +1. One clean transfer per turn.

augments.name: Pain Split
augments.effect: When you Absorb, you can split: half the stacks transfer to you, half are destroyed. Round in the condition's favor (Bleed 3 → 2 to you, 1 destroyed).

augments.name: Contamination Aura
augments.effect: Passive. Enemies that hit you in melee automatically gain Poison 1 (1 damage/round, stacks with repeated hits). Your blood is toxic — hurt you and it gets on them.

augments.name: Redirect
augments.effect: 2 AP, Medium range, Interface check vs target's Grit: remove one condition from yourself and apply it to an enemy at full strength. +1 Toxicity. The core offensive cycle.

augments.name: Tox Channel
augments.effect: 1 AP: spend 3 Toxicity to heal an ally within Close range for 1d6 + Interface bonus HP. Your contamination becomes medicine. Toxicity goes down, their HP goes up.

augments.name: Purge
augments.effect: 1 AP: destroy one condition you are currently carrying. Costs 2 Toxicity. The condition is gone — not redirected, just burned out. Emergency valve when overloaded.

augments.name: Empathic Range Extension
augments.effect: Absorb range increases from Close to Medium. Support from the backline. No more standing in melee to heal.

augments.name: Deep Cleanse
augments.effect: 2 AP: Absorb Toxicity from an ally. Remove up to your Interface bonus in Toxicity from them. You gain the same amount (+1 per Absorb on top). During downtime: treat a Scar without medical equipment (4 hours, +5 Toxicity to you).

augments.name: Mass Absorb
augments.effect: 3 AP: Absorb one condition from EVERY ally within Medium range simultaneously. +1 Toxicity per condition absorbed. Expensive but cleans the whole squad in one action.

augments.name: Suffering Echo
augments.effect: When you Absorb a condition, you learn what caused it and gain +2 to resist that condition type for the rest of the encounter. Knowledge through pain.

augments.name: Festering Redirect
augments.effect: Conditions you Redirect onto enemies tick at 150% value (round up). Bleed 2 ticks for 3 damage/round. Your suffering ferments inside you — what comes out is worse than what went in.

augments.name: Curse Touch
augments.effect: 1 AP, Close range, Interface check vs Grit: apply Hex 2 to target (-2 to all checks for 2 rounds). +1 Toxicity. Stronger than Afflict's minor effects.

augments.name: Toxic Redirect
augments.effect: When you Redirect a condition onto an enemy, also apply Poison 1 to them automatically (no check needed). Your contamination is contagious — the condition AND the poison travel together.

augments.name: Backlash
augments.effect: When you Purge a condition from yourself, apply it to the nearest enemy within Close range at half strength. Your purge is offensive. Bleed 4 purged → Bleed 2 applied to enemy.

augments.name: Karmic Loop
augments.effect: When you Absorb a condition (Martyr) and Redirect a condition (Vessel) in the same turn: heal yourself for 1d4 HP. The cycle completes cleanly. Rewards the full pump action.

augments.name: Living Antidote
augments.effect: Passive aura. Allies within Close range automatically reduce Poison, Bleed, and Toxicity accumulation by 1 per round. Your presence is a passive cleanse field.

augments.name: Martyrdom Protocol
augments.effect: Reaction: when an ally within Medium range takes damage, absorb half the damage (you take it, bypasses your armor). +2 Toxicity. Uses your Reaction.

augments.name: Sacrifice
augments.effect: Once per encounter: when an ally within Medium range would drop to 0 HP, they stay at 1 HP instead. You take the killing damage (bypasses your armor). You chose this.

augments.name: Karmic Return
augments.effect: 3 AP: release every condition you are currently carrying. Each condition is applied to an enemy of your choice within Medium range (split across targets or stack on one). +2 Toxicity per condition released. Once per encounter. The dump truck.

augments.name: Plague Touch
augments.effect: 1 AP, Close range, Interface check vs Grit: apply Burn 1d4 (1d4 damage/round for 3 rounds, ignores 1 armor). +2 Toxicity. The most aggressive DoT in the Sin Eater's kit.

augments.name: Condition Siphon
augments.effect: 2 AP, Medium range, Interface check (DC 8): remove one buff or positive condition from an enemy (regeneration, damage boost, shielding). The buff is destroyed. +1 Toxicity. Strip the boss's advantages.

augments.name: Equilibrium Engine
augments.effect: Your Toxicity penalties don't apply until Toxicity 10 instead of 6. Maximum HP permanently reduced by 3. The sweet spot widens at the cost of your ceiling. Run hotter, break easier.

augments.name: Vessel of All Sorrows
augments.effect: Passive. You cannot collapse from Toxicity (still take penalties and HP drain at high levels, but Toxicity 20+ no longer forces unconsciousness or a Scar). Your Absorb costs +0 Toxicity at all times. When you Redirect a condition, it is applied at 150% strength (stacks with Festering Redirect for 200% if you have both). All DoT conditions you apply last 1 additional round. You are an endless conduit — suffering flows through you without limit.

content.text: Synergy Examples
Pure Martyr Build

Path: Empathic Link → Rift Metabolizer → Sympathetic Healing → Transfer Efficiency → Empathic Range Extension → Deep Cleanse → Living Antidote + Sacrifice. Every turn: Absorb conditions from whoever needs it most, heal them for 1d4+Interface in the process. Deep Cleanse handles Toxicity on allies. Living Antidote passively reduces the squad's condition accumulation. Sacrifice is the emergency button.
Pure Vessel Build

Path: Empathic Link → Afflict Touch → Contamination Aura → Redirect → Festering Redirect → Toxic Redirect → Karmic Return + Plague Touch. Absorb conditions as fuel, Redirect them at 150% strength with bonus Poison stacked on. Contamination Aura poisons anything that melees you. Plague Touch burns priority targets. Karmic Return dumps everything onto the boss.
Hybrid (The Full Sin Eater — The Pump)

Path: Empathic Link → Transfer Efficiency → Pain Split → Redirect → Contamination Aura → Festering Redirect → Karmic Loop → Equilibrium Engine. Absorb from allies (half stacks via Pain Split, free first transfer). Redirect onto enemies (150% strength, bonus Poison). Karmic Loop heals you 1d4 when you complete the cycle. Equilibrium Engine widens your safe zone to Toxicity 10. Every turn is: eat, process, redirect.
Sin Eater + Leech Cross-Tree

Combo: Sin Eater T2 (Transfer Efficiency, Redirect) + Leech T2 (Furnace Core, Burn). The Leech generates Toxicity for personal power. The Sin Eater uses Deep Cleanse to pull Toxicity off the Leech and Redirects absorbed conditions onto enemies. The Leech is the engine, the Sin Eater is the exhaust system.
Sin Eater + Influence Cross-Tree

Combo: Sin Eater T2 (Sympathetic Healing, Transfer Efficiency) + Influence T2 (Inspiring Presence, Tactical HUD Link). Double support stack. Influence buffs accuracy and calls targets. Sin Eater cleanses conditions and applies DoTs. The ultimate support duo.
Sin Eater + Fortress Cross-Tree

Combo: Sin Eater T2 (Empathic Link, Sympathetic Healing) + Fortress T2 (Subdermal Plating, damage reduction). The unkillable healer. High armor means you survive what you Absorb longer. Martyrdom Protocol lets you soak ally damage that your armor reduces.
Sin Eater + Shadow Cross-Tree

Combo: Shadow T2 (Sprint Actuators, Momentum Drive) + Sin Eater T2 (Afflict Touch, Contamination Aura). The plague runner. Charge into enemies (Shadow Pinball), hit them (apply Poison via Venomous Intent or Contamination Aura), 5 free feet, charge the next one. Every target you touch starts ticking.
Design Notes

Why no direct damage: The Sin Eater's offensive identity is DoT — Bleed, Poison, Burn, Hex ticking on enemies over multiple rounds. This is thematically perfect (suffering is slow, not explosive) and mechanically distinct from every other tree. The enemy doesn't die in a flashy moment — they die because three different conditions have been ticking for four rounds.

Why Toxicity as mana: Every other support class uses AP as their primary cost. Sin Eater adds Toxicity as a second constraint. The Negotiator asks "what do I spend AP on?" The Sin Eater asks "what do I spend AP on AND how high can my Toxicity climb?" Two constraints instead of one.

Why every ability costs something: Absorb costs Toxicity. Tox Channel costs Toxicity. Emergency Transfusion costs HP. Redirect costs Toxicity and AP. Nothing is free. This prevents passive heal-botting. Every heal is a decision: "Can I afford this Toxicity? Can I afford this HP? Will this push me into the danger zone?"

Why Festering Redirect at T3: This is the Vessel's damage amplifier. At T3, the Sin Eater has learned to ferment suffering — what comes out is worse than what went in. 150% tick values means the Absorb → Redirect cycle isn't just neutral transfer, it's amplification. Bleed 2 absorbed becomes Bleed 3 on the enemy.

Why the mastery removes Toxicity collapse: Vessel of All Sorrows solves the fundamental ceiling problem. At T5, Toxicity 20+ no longer forces collapse. You still take -3 to checks and -2 HP/round at high levels. The limiting factor shifts from "mana overflow" to "can my body survive the throughput."

Why Plague Carrier at T4: +1 stack on every DoT you apply means: Afflict Touch's Bleed 1 becomes Bleed 2. Withering Touch's Bleed 1d4 becomes Bleed 1d4+1. Over a 5-round fight, that's enormous extra ticking damage.

content.label: AP Math — Sin Eater Turns
content.text: • The Pump: Absorb Bleed off Berserker (1 AP, +1 Tox) → Redirect Bleed onto enemy at 150% (2 AP, +1 Tox) → Tox Channel to heal Phantom (1 AP, -3 Tox) → Move (1 AP). Net Toxicity: -1. • Emergency: Mass Absorb all allies (3 AP, +4 Tox) → Purge worst condition (1 AP, +2 Tox) → carrying 3 conditions, Toxicity spiked by 6. Next turn MUST Redirect. • DoT Pressure: Withering Touch on A (1 AP, +1 Tox) → Afflict Touch on B (1 AP, +1 Tox) → Redirect carried condition onto C (2 AP, +1 Tox) → Move (1 AP). Three enemies ticking. • The Gambit: Sin Eater's Gambit (4 AP — absorb everything, redistribute onto enemies) → 1 AP left for movement. Nuclear option. • Sustained Heal: Tox Channel (1 AP, -3 Tox) → Wellspring (2 AP, -4 Tox) → Move (1 AP) → Empathic Touch (1 AP, +1 Tox). Net Toxicity: -6. Pure healing turn.
